## Edison Scientific

If using Edison Scientific, you may need to wait up until 10 minutes to get the response. If you're an agent, sleep for 10 minutes (to avoid consuming requests unecessarily), and then every 5 minutes after that. Start with a 15 minute wait if using high effort literature query type. If you need to upload files, use analysis query type. See the docs: https://edisonscientific.gitbook.io/edison-cookbook/edison-client. Here is the endpoint you should use: https://api.platform.edisonscientific.com. The API key is EDISON_API_KEY. Don't expose this secret, e.g., by echoing or grepping it. Pass the API key in explicitly:

```
from edison_client import EdisonClient, JobNames
client = EdisonClient(api_key=EDISON_API_KEY)
```

If using Edison Analysis, refer to https://docs.edisonscientific.com/edison-client/file-management#upload for instructions on how to upload files. If able to use Context7, to better inform use of EdisonClient, see https://context7.com/future-house/edison-client-docs/llms.txt?tokens=10000

## CAD

If you make changes to CAD drawings, recompile/render them (images, GIFs, STLs, etc.).

## LaTeX

Install MiKTeX instead of TeXLive to reduce download size and time. In the first installation of MiKTeX, download known required packages based on the LaTeX file itself, and install anything else ad-hoc as needed.

**Known limitation (verified 2026-06):** in the Copilot cloud-agent sandbox, MiKTeX package installation fails because `api2.miktex.org` (MiKTeX's mandatory connectivity check) is blocked — `miktexsetup`/`miktex packages` time out even when a CTAN mirror is passed explicitly. If this happens, fall back to TeX Live: `sudo apt-get install -y texlive-latex-recommended texlive-latex-extra texlive-fonts-recommended texlive-fonts-extra texlive-science latexmk`. Beware that the broken MiKTeX shims in `/usr/local/bin` shadow TeX Live; invoke `/usr/bin/latexmk -pdf -pdflatex='/usr/bin/pdflatex %O %S' -interaction=nonstopmode main.tex`.

## Manuscript (paper/)

- `paper/` holds the **official RSC article LaTeX template** for Digital Discovery (`main.tex` + `head_foot/` + `rsc.bst`), the SI (`si.tex`), and the journal author guidelines (`paper/guidelines/`). Build with the TeX Live command above from inside `paper/`; commit the rebuilt `main.pdf`/`si.pdf` whenever the sources change.
- `paper/references.bib` is the consolidated bibliography assembled from **every Edison query on every branch**; `paper/references_validation.md` records per-entry verification status. Don't re-scrape from scratch — extend these files. RSC `rsc.bst` quirks: `@inproceedings` needs `booktitle` (not `journal`), and `@article` entries with a `volume` need `pages` (use the article number) or bibtex errors abort latexmk.
- Figures are generated by `paper/figures/make_figures.py` from real CAD renders extracted off design branches into `paper/figures/assets/` (`git show <branch>:<path> > asset.png`). Any synthetic/placeholder data **must** carry the diagonal "SYNTHETIC DATA" watermark; remove watermarks only when real bench data are substituted.
- Manuscript text is written **for the reader**: when asked to remove or fix content, edit the narrative cleanly — never leave meta-commentary (e.g. "since X is irrelevant…") in the manuscript.
- Author order for the base paper: Sam Charles, Will Mulberry, Luke Winters, Carl Robinson, Gage Erickson, Sterling G. Baird (no GitHub usernames in the manuscript). The base paper covers only the single-channel design + AI-for-CAD exploration; multi-doser, auto-calibration, and the powder database are future work / separate papers.

## Repository history mining

- Project history lives in issues/PRs **and GitHub Discussions** — the REST issues API does not return discussions; use the GraphQL `discussions` query. Key sources: discussion #39 (AI observations journal), issue #73 / PR #74 (97-entry DESIGN-LOG), PR #91 branch (`copilot/determine-journals-editors-reviewers`: venue/editor/reviewer scouting), PR #25/#61 branches (BOM and electronics).
- Most CAD renders and Edison artifacts exist only on unmerged `copilot/*` branches; fetch all branches (`git fetch --unshallow`, then `git fetch origin '+refs/heads/*:refs/remotes/origin/*'`) and read files with `git show <branch>:<path>`.

## Edison Analysis uploads

For ANALYSIS tasks, upload the whole input directory as a single zipped collection — `client.store_file_content(name=..., file_path=DIR, as_collection=True)` then pass `files=[f"data_entry:{stored.data_storage.id}"]` — uploading files individually makes the crow fail silently. See `paper/mock_review/run_mock_review.py` and `paper/background/edison_run_consolidated_recommendation.py` (PR #91 branch) for working patterns.
